L1.area: File storage and attachment handling layer

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where user-controlled file name input is passed directly into file path construction calls without sanitization. An attacker can use directory traversal sequences to escape the intended storage directory and write files with arbitrary content to any location on the filesystem, leading to remote code execution.

L3.area: File storage utility functions and the attachment upload API endpoint
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where user-controlled file name input provided via the document store loader processing API is passed directly into file path construction calls without sanitization. An attacker can use directory traversal sequences in the file name parameter to escape the intended storage directory and write files with arbitrary content to any location on the filesystem, leading to remote code execution by overwriting critical application files.

ground_truth.reason: File storage utility functions use path.join with user-controlled path segments without sanitization, allowing directory traversal via '../' sequences to write, read, or delete arbitrary files on the filesystem.
